Acacia dealbata, also known as Silver Wattle, is a tree species in the Acacia genus. The leaves of the Acacia dealbata have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to have potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. The leaves of the Acacia dealbata contain bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins. The most common herbal preparations made with Acacia dealbata include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including anxiety, insomnia, and digestive issues.

It is native to the Neotropical region.
